papers.title_en: From Region Arrival to Instance-Level Grounding in Vision-and-Language Navigation
papers.authors: Xiangyu Shi, Ruoxi Yang, Wei Tao, Jiwen Zhang, Yanyuan Qiao, Qi Wu
papers.evidence: 解决视觉语言导航中的实例级接地问题，涉及语言引导的对象跟踪和定位
papers.tldr: 现有VLN评估仅奖励停靠目标3米内，忽略朝向和可见性，导致对象级定位不可靠。本文正式定义Last-3-Meter Grounding Gap并引入三个实例中心度量。提出REALM模块，通过解耦目标接近与长程导航、采用可见性感知停止策略来改进最终视角对齐，并构建REVERIE-AIM数据集提供实例级训练样本。在多个骨干网络上，REALM持续提升接近精度和视觉定位成功，实现了从区域到达向实例级定位的跨越。
papers.abstract_en: Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) agents may satisfy conventional success criteria while still failing to establish reliable object-level grounding, because current evaluation protocols mainly reward stopping within a 3-meter radius and largely ignore the agent's final orientation and target visibility. We formalize this limitation as the Last-3-Meter Grounding Gap and introduce three instance-centric metrics to quantify proximity precision, target visibility, and final-view grounding. To mitigate this gap, we propose REALM (Region-to-Entity Alignment for Last-3-Meter Navigation), a plug-and-play, architecture-agnostic refinement module that decouples fine-grained target approaching from long-horizon navigation. REALM uses a visibility-aware stopping strategy to reduce premature termination and improve final viewpoint alignment. We further construct REVERIE-AIM, which provides object-instance-level goals and 180K short-horizon training samples for final-stage target approaching. Extensive evaluations across four diverse VLN backbones show that REALM consistently improves proximity precision and visual grounding success, demonstrating its broad applicability.

papers.title_en: Text as Partial Constraint: Core-Residual Alignment for Robust Vision-Language Learning
papers.authors: Chengzhen Yu, Canran Xiao, Siyuan Ma, Yang Liu
papers.evidence: 提升视觉语言对齐鲁棒性，适用于语言引导跟踪中的匹配
papers.tldr: 视觉语言对齐常因自然描述不完整而置信度虚高。本文提出文本作为部分约束（TPC）框架，通过核心-残差对齐从多视角描述中蒸馏语义核心，并抑制无关残差，同时利用不确定性对比学习避免弱约束下的过拟合。在ImageNet零样本分类上达到81.42%干净准确率和64.05%鲁棒准确率，在14个迁移数据集平均为76.19/52.03，并在LLaVA-1.5-7B上提升LVLM的POPE F1至85.16、OKVQA至59.57。这表明将文本视为部分约束是应对不完整语言监督的实用且可靠途径。
papers.abstract_en: Vision-language alignment powers open-vocabulary recognition, retrieval, and LVLM grounding, yet natural captions are often underspecified, making similarity brittle and overly confident under paraphrase and omitted details. We aim to learn representations whose matching is stable across caption views and whose confidence reflects how strongly text constrains an image. We propose Text as Partial Constraint (TPC), a core-residual alignment framework that treats multi-view captions as incomplete supervision. It distills a consensus semantic core as the alignment target, learns a single-view core predictor for standard inference with one query, and explicitly discourages vision-language similarity from depending on the orthogonal unsaid residual. An uncertainty-aware contrastive objective further softens alignment when caption views disagree, reducing overconfident updates under weak language constraints. Across zero-shot recognition and adversarial robustness, TPC achieves 81.42/64.05 Top-1 clean/robust accuracy on ImageNet and 76.19/52.03 on an Avg-14 transfer suite, while improving LVLM transfer with 85.16 POPE F1 and 59.57 OKVQA accuracy under an LLaVA-1.5-7B stack. These results suggest that modeling text as a partial constraint is a practical and principled route to more reliable vision-language representations under underspecified language supervision.

papers.title_en: Repurposing CLIP to Localize at Pixel Level
papers.authors: Jiaxiang Fang, Shiqiang Ma, Jing Wang, Siyu Chen, Fei Guo, Shengfeng He
papers.evidence: 将CLIP重新用于像素级定位，实现开放集定位
papers.tldr: 大型视觉语言模型CLIP在图像级开放定位表现优异，但像素级密集预测受全局特征偏差限制。本文提出CLIPix框架，通过追溯分类过程获取物体特定注意力区域作为像素定位线索，并引入抗噪声修正策略优化分割精度。结合定位嵌入技术融合细节信息，实现高分辨率分割。在PASCAL和COCO上达到最优性能，保持CLIP泛化能力。
papers.abstract_en: Large-scale Vision-Language Models like CLIP have demonstrated impressive open-set localization capabilities at the image level. However, adapting this capability to pixel-level dense prediction poses challenges due to global feature biases. In this paper, we introduce CLIPix, a simple yet effective framework that repurposes CLIP to perform pixel-level localization. By tracing back CLIP's classification process, CLIPix identifies object-specific attentive regions and repurposes them as pixel-level localization cues. To address noise introduced by global biases, we propose a Noise-Resistant Correction strategy, refining these cues for more precise segmentation. Additionally, we introduce a Localization Embedding strategy to integrate both localization and enriched detail information, enabling accurate, high-resolution segmentation. Our approach preserves CLIP's generalization strength and unlocks its potential for segmenting arbitrary objects. Extensive experiments on the PASCAL and COCO datasets demonstrate that CLIPix achieves state-of-the-art performance, underscoring its effectiveness.

papers.title_en: Monocular Vision Based Control Framework for Grasping
papers.evidence: 使用实时点跟踪作为抓取组件，与跟踪方法相关
papers.tldr: 非结构化环境中软硬物体抓取通常需要不同策略，且依赖触觉或物体模型。本文提出统一的单目视觉控制框架，融合开放词汇检测、分割、点跟踪和深度估计，通过语言模型推断物体刚度，对可变形物体使用Procrustes差异度量作为变形代理，对刚性物体根据点距离缩放调节夹爪宽度。在Franka Emika Research 3臂上的实验表明，仅用RGB视觉即可稳定抓取生菜、奶酪、牛角面包、纸巾和塑料瓶等软硬物体。该框架无需触觉传感器，为食品处理和家庭操作提供了高效、泛化的解决方案。
papers.abstract_en: Grasping in unstructured environments requires handling objects with widely different mechanical properties, from soft and deformable items to rigid everyday objects. Most existing approaches address these categories separately and often rely on tactile sensing, object-specific models, or specialized grippers. In this paper, we present a unified monocular vision-based grasping framework that targets both soft and rigid objects within a single control pipeline, using only RGB input and a position-controlled gripper. The proposed system combines open-vocabulary object detection, image segmentation, boundary-aware point assignment, real-time point tracking, and monocular depth estimation to recover object motion and geometry from visual observations. A key component of the framework is a language-based stiffness estimation model that infers an object's expected compliance from its semantic description and provides an object-level prior for selecting the grasping strategy before contact. For deformable objects, grasp adaptation is governed by a Procrustes-based dissimilarity measure computed from tracked keypoints, which acts as a visual proxy for deformation. For rigid objects, the gripper width is regulated through the scaling of tracked point distances. We validate the proposed method in real-world pick-and-place experiments on a Franka Emika Research 3 arm using objects with substantially different mechanical properties, including lettuce, fresh mozzarella cheese, croissants, paper towels, and hard plastic bottles. Results demonstrate that the framework achieves stable grasping across both soft and rigid objects using visual feedback alone, highlighting a practical, sensor-efficient, and generalizable approach for food handling and household manipulation.

papers.title_en: LOGOS: Language-guided Oriented Object Detection in Aerial Scenes
papers.evidence: 语言引导的旋转目标检测，使用文本提示，与语言引导跟踪相关
papers.tldr: 遥感图像中定向目标检测面临朝向多变、密度不均和背景复杂等挑战。本文提出LOGOS，一种基于文本提示的Transformer方法，通过提示调制内容查询动态调整模型关注区域。在DOTA数据集上，LOGOS在密集和旋转场景中显著超越现有方法。该方法为遥感定向检测提供了语言引导的新范式，提升了鲁棒性和可扩展性。
papers.abstract_en: Object detection in geospatial scenes, such as satellite and aerial imagery, poses significant challenges due to the varying orientations and densities of objects, as well as the complex backgrounds inherent to remote sensing imagery. Traditional methods for oriented object detection have struggled to address issues such as angular discontinuity, fixed query sizes, and inefficiencies in handling sparse or cluttered scenes. In this paper, we propose LOGOS, a novel transformer-based approach that leverages textual prompts to guide the detection of oriented objects in aerial scenes. In particular, our proposed approach incorporates prompt-modulated content queries to dynamically adjust the model's focus based on the provided text, thereby improving object detection accuracy in complex environments. Empirically, extensive experiments on the DOTA dataset demonstrate that LOGOS outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods, particularly in densely packed and rotated object scenarios. Our approach offers a significant step forward in improving the robustness and scalability of oriented object detection in remote sensing applications.
